summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Owner Dashboard feature. Backend: 21/21 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All tabs working correctly. Fixed 1 bug in error handling for access denied state.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED - Access denied error not displayed when regular user accessed /owner route
ui_bugs.fix_applied: Added throw err to re-throw error after setting error state so it propagates to caller

critical_code_review_comments: All billing endpoints working: /api/billing/plans (4 plans), /api/billing/credits/packages (4 packages)
All admin endpoints working with owner auth: /api/admin/stats, /api/admin/users, /api/admin/projects, /api/admin/billing/overview
Health check returns 50 engines including billing_engine and admin_engine
Owner access control working - regular users get 403 on admin endpoints
Frontend Owner Dashboard renders correctly at /owner route with all 4 tabs (Overview, Users, Projects, Billing)
Users tab shows table with role, plan, status columns
Projects tab shows table with owner, type, status columns
Billing tab shows revenue metrics and transactions
seed_data_creation: Owner user already exists in database
context_for_next_testing_agent: Owner Dashboard fully tested and working. Backend has 50 engines including billing_engine and admin_engine. Billing routes support Stripe integration for subscriptions and credit purchases. Admin routes require owner auth (email in OWNER_EMAILS env var or role=owner/admin). Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_owner_dashboard.py covers all billing and admin endpoints.

frontend_ui_tests.overview_tab: PASS - Shows stats cards (Total Users, Projects, Sessions, Credits)
frontend_ui_tests.users_tab: PASS - Shows user table with Role, Plan, Status columns
frontend_ui_tests.projects_tab: PASS - Shows project table with Owner, Type, Status columns
frontend_ui_tests.billing_tab: PASS - Shows revenue metrics and transactions
frontend_ui_tests.access_denied_regular_user: PASS (after fix) - Shows Access Denied message

bug_fixed.reason: Error was caught but not re-thrown, so the outer try-catch in loadData never saw the error and didn't set loading=false with error state properly displayed

mocked_status.note: All billing and admin endpoints are live with real database queries
